name: blender
description: Connect Artyx to Blender via the official Blender Lab MCP server for scene inspection, bpy automation, materials, animation, rendering, and verification loops.

interface.shortDescription: Build and animate 3D scenes in a live Blender session.
interface.longDescription: Drive a connected Blender session over the official Blender Lab MCP server: inspect blend files and objects, execute bpy code, query the bundled Python API docs, capture viewport screenshots, and verify every change. Artyx connects over HTTP to a locally running blender-mcp process that relays to the Blender MCP add-on.
defaultPrompt: Build a small treasure-room scene in Blender and animate the chest lid opening over 30 frames.

companion.title: Finish setup inside Blender
companion.summary: Install the official Blender Lab MCP add-on and blender-mcp server, then run it in HTTP mode.
steps: In Blender, add the Blender Lab extensions repository https://lab.blender.org/ and install+enable the "MCP" add-on (its preferences panel configures host/port and optional auto-start).
Install the official MCP server: pip install "git+https://projects.blender.org/lab/blender_mcp.git#subdirectory=mcp" (requires Python 3.10+).
Run it in HTTP mode: blender-mcp --transport http --port 8000 (any free port; enter the same port when Artyx asks during install).
